title: "Automatically follow detected online state" does not work for Yahoo SMTP server
creator: ndbrill
content:
Cannot get online mode to send message. It is NOT a matter of a menu item hidden.

"Automatically follow detected online state" does not work for Yahoo SMTP server after computer turned off and Internet connection lost. And now no pop up appears to add a newly acquired Yahoo password in order to send a message.

After computer turned off, SMTP always requires a new 3rd party password from Yahoo. IMAP never requires updated password from Yahoo.

title: Authentication Errors on Launch
creator: pixelmoses
content:
Recently tried to switch back to using Daily, but now I get authentication errors each time I start the program. It's always the following three errors in sequence: Authentication failure while connecting to server imap.gmail.com. The certificate for imap.gmail.com does not come from a trusted source. Non-overridable TLS error occurred. Handshake error or probably the TLS version or certificate used by server imap.gmail.com is incompatible.

I've tried getting rid of all of my saved passwords and even completely getting rid of the account and re-adding it. I've also tried completely disabling my VPN's service in Windows to see if that was somehow affecting it, but nothing.

title: Clean install of thunderbird but no profile found in usual location. I put in some old profiles, but still doesn't recognize them. Multiple tries.
creator: jmcla
content: I've used thunderbird for years. Now I can't get it back. I tried a clean uninstall/reinstall. Downloaded from the thunderbird site. Seems to install fine, but then says no profile recognized. I put some old profiles in the appdata\roaming\thunderbird\profiles in desperation, but still no luck. My clean uninstall did involve registry entries (Revo-Uninstall). So I'd like to get a clean start with Thunderbird, but it won't let me. Any suggestions greatly appreciated. I dread the idea of using another email after all these years!
